§6Catalytic Reformer§f

§aUpgrade petroleum fractions into more valuable products — reformate, liquid hydrogen, and more!§f

The §bCatalytic Reformer§f is an optional late-game machine that converts certain fluids into more valuable ones. It is known primarily for producing §bReformate§f and §bLiquid Hydrogen§f as byproducts. It consumes §e400 kHE/s§f and §e2,000 mB/s§f of input fluid, and requires a §bCatalytic Converter§f to operate.

§6Crafting§f (Assembly Factory):
§b• §e12×§f Cast Steel Plate
§b• §e8×§f Copper Plate
§b• §e8×§f Niobium Ingot
§b• §e4×§f Cadmium Steel Ingot
§b• §e3×§f Steel Shell
§b• §e8×§f Steel Pipe
§b• §e1×§f Motor
§b• §e1×§f Versatile Circuit Board

§6Catalytic Converter§f (Crafting Table):
§b• §e2×§f Hard Plastic Bar
§b• §e1×§f Cobalt Powder
§b• §e1×§f Arsenic Ingot

§6Primary Recipe§f (Naphtha):
§b• §e1,000mB§f Naphtha → §e500mB§f Reformate + §e150mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e100mB§f Liquid Hydrogen.

§6Other Inputs§f:
§b• Heating Oil§f → §e500mB§f Naphtha + §e150mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e100mB§f Liquid Hydrogen.
§b• Light Oil§f → §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e150mB§f Liquid Hydrogen + §e100mB§f Reformate Gas.
§b• Sour Gas§f → §e750mB§f Sulfuric Acid + §e150mB§f Liquid Hydrogen + §e100mB§f Petroleum Gas.

§eTip§f: The reformer pairs extremely well with the §bHydrotreater§f — both produce Liquid Hydrogen. The Liquid Hydrogen output can be used for hydrotreating, creating a closed-loop system with your vacuum refinery setup!
